title: Build "Causal Merge Verdict" (deterministic v1) as the next game-changer
context: A 50-agent game-changer workflow diverged bold concepts across 6 strategic lenses, validated demand/competitors on the web, and adversarially scored each on a moat-weighted rubric. Causal Merge Verdict scored highest (76; differentiation 82). The synthesizer instead recommended Constraint P&L (64), but that concept's headline 'mistakes prevented' ROI rests on an unobservable counterfactual — the pre-edit hook is fire-and-forget with no post-edit callback — so I overrode it. User confirmed Causal Merge Verdict.
decision: Ship a DETERMINISTIC v1 only: a 'causal verdict' over a diff that, for each flagged invariant, joins constraint → source_decision (the why) → bug lineage (the failure that motivated it) and emits one cited verdict (pass | warn | block). Block iff a blocking invariant is directly hit OR a deliberately-retired symbol/dep tied to a blocking invariant is re-added (reuse checkConstraints + strictgate isStrictBlocker + the Regression Guard). Expose as a new MCP tool hunch_merge_verdict(base) callable by any assistant before opening/merging a PR, and enrich the existing hunch check / CI guard output with the causal citations. EXPLICITLY DEFER the LLM 'intent-drift / decision-contradiction' legs — that is where a blocking gate's precision collapses (the concept's main kill-risk).
consequences: Differentiation = citing the WHY (decision+bug lineage) a diff-only reviewer (CodeRabbit/Greptile) structurally cannot, NOT a smarter LLM judge.
Avoid false-positive gate decay: only the deterministic legs block; ambiguous/causal-narrative parts are advisory context.
Cold-start: a fresh repo has no retired signals/bug lineage, so verdicts are sparse until history accrues — acceptable; reuse backfill.
alternatives_rejected: Constraint P&L — ROI number rests on an unobservable counterfactual (no post-edit callback); moat IS trust in that number
Memory Federation — strong moat narrative but zero day-one pull (needs repo #2 + teammates) and unbuilt cross-repo ID-rewrite/semantic-scope primitives
LLM intent-drift legs of Causal Merge Verdict — precision collapses where a blocking gate cannot afford it
